name: Workspace Fixes + Permission System
description: Foundational fixes discovered through real usage: Pi CWD follows workspace changes, file tree auto-refreshes after agent writes, persistent permission cache with yolo mode.
notes: Phase 6 (Wire Pi Features) is complete — these are gaps found through actual use.
Permission system follows the same .tide/ persistence pattern as tags.
Yolo mode auto-approves all tool calls — equivalent to Pi's native yolo mode.
File explorer auto-refresh triggers on write/edit/create/delete tool completions.
Must complete before Phase 7 (Router + Task Classification).

tasks.title: Fix Pi CWD on Workspace Change
tasks.description: When user opens a folder via Open Folder, the Pi process keeps running with its original CWD (often Desktop). Fix: after openWorkspace() updates the stored root, call restartPi() to respawn Pi with the correct CWD. The restart_pi command already reads workspace_root from AppState.

tasks.title: File Explorer Auto-Refresh on Agent Writes
tasks.description: File tree doesn't update when Pi creates/modifies/deletes files. Listen for tool_execution_end events where tool name matches write/edit/create/delete. Trigger workspace store refreshFileTree() which re-calls fsListDir and updates the tree.

tasks.title: Persistent Permission Cache
tasks.description: New permissionStore.ts with Permission interface (id, toolName, scope, pattern, decision). Persisted to .tide/permissions.json via Rust IPC (permissions_load/permissions_save). ApprovalDialog gets 'Remember' dropdown: remember per-tool or per-file. approvalStore checks cache before showing dialog — auto-responds if matched.

tasks.title: Yolo Mode Toggle
tasks.description: Add yoloMode boolean to permissionStore, persisted in .tide/permissions.json. When enabled, all confirm requests auto-approved without showing dialog. Safety settings panel (SafetyPlaceholder.tsx) replaced with real UI: yolo toggle, saved permissions list with remove/clear actions.

tasks.title: Keyboard Shortcuts Panel in Settings
tasks.description: New 'Shortcuts' tab in Settings modal listing all keyboard shortcuts grouped by category (General, Editor, Agent Panel, Dialogs). Read-only reference panel so users can discover available shortcuts.
